pages.raw_text:
Now that the winter is here and
work has shut down all over the
city, the employment agencies
which resort to grating the poor
working man out his last few dol.
lars by selling him fake jobs, are
in their element, and are doing a
flourishing business.


Hardly a day passes but from 20
to 80 unfortunates who have been
grated by the taking agencies call
at the mayors office and show en-
ployment agency tickets upon
which they have gone out of the
city to take jobs which either did
not exist or were not as repre-
scented.


A case in point is one where a
number of men were sent out by
the All Nations Employment
agency this week, to take jobs as
sawyers in logging camp nearly
100 miles from the city.


The men paid 81 each for their
jobs. They went to the place
where they were to have gone to
work, but found that there was
nothing doing for sawyers. They
were told that they could go to
work for a few days building
roads, the hardest work around a
logging camp. They refused, how
ever, as they were experienced
sawyers, and had been specifically
sent out to take sawyers' jobs.


The foreman on the work signed
their tickets showing that he had
not hired them-but he enabled
the employment agency to escape
refunding their money by adding a
notation to the effect that he had
offered the men jobs at road build
ing.


So when the men who had been
buncoed came back to town they
were given the laugh by the en-
ployment men.
